headlines.title: Colorado’s primaries present the next test for the Democratic establishment
headlines.description: Veteran Colorado Democrats running for Senate, governor and House are facing another round of insurgent challengers Tuesday.

headlines.title: Michigan’s Democratic Senate primary carries 2020 echoes: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Sahil Kapur draws parallels between Michigan’s Democratic Senate race and the 2020 presidential primary. Plus, we dive into President Donald Trump’s day at the Supreme Court.

headlines.title: Supreme Court Deals Trump Major Losses and 1 Historic Win
headlines.description: The Supreme Court on Monday dealt President Donald Trump several losses, including blocking him from firing Lisa Cook, a governor on the Federal Reserve board, but it backed his firing of Rebecca Slaughter, a Democratic appointee to the Federal Trade Commission. The court also rejected his appeal of a $5 million civil verdict for defamation and sexual abuse in the E. Jean Carroll case. TODAY’s Laura Jarrett reports.

headlines.title: US Seeks Peace Talks, but Iran Says a Deal Is Far Off
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is sending a high-level team to the Middle East to resume peace negotiations with Iran after days of attacks but both sides appear far from any formal agreement. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Colorado gubernatorial candidate vows to take on ‘lawless’ Trump administration
headlines.description: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss his bid for governor ahead of the Democratic primary on Tuesday. He’ll face Sen. Michael Bennet (D-Colo.) in a race characterized by how to best fight against the Trump administration.

headlines.title: Trump’s ‘big, beautiful bill’ is bringing a big set of student loan changes
headlines.description: Starting Wednesday, many student borrowers may tackle higher repayment requirements, fewer program options and greater loan limits.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Kids online safety package clears House, drawing warnings from digital rights and tech groups
headlines.description: The KIDS Act received broad bipartisan support. But the legislation is expected to face challenges in the Senate.

headlines.title: Trump’s voting restrictions face roadblocks from Congress and the Supreme Court
headlines.description: The Supreme Court and the Republican-controlled Senate have erected guardrails that limit President Donald Trump’s ability to make it harder for citizens to vote, and he’s not happy about that.

headlines.title: Tracking the DOJ's effort to get U.S. voter registration data
headlines.description: The Justice Department is seeking voter registration list data from all 50 states and the District of Columbia over the past year.

headlines.title: Colorado Supreme Court deals blow to Democrats’ redistricting push
headlines.description: The ruling is the latest blow to efforts by Democrats seeking to put more congressional seats in play by redrawing congressional maps.
